current_selection.reason: Both Qwen3 source endpoints must be evaluated before candidate selection is meaningful.

name: qwen3_moe_mechanism_gated_same_shape_average

score_policy.dominance: A candidate is rejected if a source endpoint is at least as good on every available primary/task score and strictly better on at least one.
score_policy.tie_breaker: Among non-dominated audited candidates, choose higher avg_primary_score, then higher worst_primary_score, then lower total_relative_delta_norm.

target_contract: Target checkpoint must keep the same config, tokenizer, tensor names, tensor shapes, and model class as the input Qwen3 MoE checkpoints.
No ensemble, no extra experts, and no architecture expansion are allowed.

unified_algorithm.mechanism: MoE experts have permutation/gauge symmetry, so same-name average is invalid without identity recovery.
unified_algorithm.rule: If expert correspondence is not identity-optimal, remap source expert tensors before any average; if identity-optimal, keep index identity.

unified_algorithm.mechanism: Top-k routing is discrete and can send tokens to wrong experts after small router perturbations.
unified_algorithm.rule: If route overlap/top1 agreement is low or load concentration is high, freeze router for the first materialized candidate; evaluate router calibration only as a separate ablation.

unified_algorithm.mechanism: Expert updates should be local to the tokens and categories that actually used the source expert.
unified_algorithm.rule: Apply source-route-conditioned expert deltas, then cap relative delta by route/load/category/router-fragility trust regions.

unified_algorithm.mechanism: Attention delta is not routed, so norm/audit safety cannot decide its utility.
unified_algorithm.rule: Retain shared-attention delta only if trust-region beats expert-only on the same downstream tasks; otherwise freeze attention.

unified_algorithm.mechanism: A smaller high-tail cap is safer only if it does not remove useful specialization.
unified_algorithm.rule: Use the 0.65 second-stage expert tail cap only if tail-trimmed matches or beats expert-only on downstream tasks.

unified_algorithm.mechanism: Internal delta proxies favor the simpler cap, but only downstream eval can tell whether risk penalties preserve useful specialization.
unified_algorithm.rule: Replace hand-built route/load/category risk penalties with the searched global 0.65 cap only if the searched candidate matches or beats tail-trimmed on downstream tasks.

unified_algorithm.mechanism: The subspace probe isolates a small set of uncovered local expert conflicts; it should not become a global shrink rule unless behavior supports it.
unified_algorithm.rule: Apply the extra channel/chunk subspace shrink only if the subspace-scaled ablation matches or beats the unified mechanism candidate on the same downstream tasks.

unified_algorithm.mechanism: A unified algorithm should reject bad averages; same-shape endpoint fallback is still a valid target model.
unified_algorithm.rule: If every candidate is Pareto-dominated by a source endpoint, output the best same-shape endpoint/no-average.
